_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PANEL
name: Luanti
author: support@pterodactyl.io
description: An open source voxel game engine (formerly Minetest). Play one of our many games, mod a game to your liking, make your own game, or play on a multiplayer server.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Luanti Installation Script
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.luanti

apt update
apt -y install curl unzip

LUANTI_SERVER_KIND=${LUANTI_SERVER_KIND:-standard}
INSTANCE_TEMPLATE_NAME=${INSTANCE_TEMPLATE_NAME:-}
INSTANCE_TEMPLATE_MOUNT=${INSTANCE_TEMPLATE_MOUNT:-/mnt/presets}

if [ "$LUANTI_SERVER_KIND" = "instance" ]; then
  TEMPLATE_DIR="${INSTANCE_TEMPLATE_MOUNT%/}/$INSTANCE_TEMPLATE_NAME"
  echo "Instance server detected. Template bootstrap will run on first container start from $TEMPLATE_DIR"
fi

# Create server.log
LOG_FILE=/mnt/server/server.log
if [ -f "$LOG_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Log file already exists."
else 
  echo "Log file does not exist. Making one..."
  touch "$LOG_FILE"
fi

# Create luanti.conf
CONFIG_FILE=/mnt/server/.luanti/luanti.conf
if [ -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Config file already exists."
else 
  echo "Config file does not exist. Making one..."
  curl -sSL -o /mnt/server/.luanti/luanti.conf.example https://raw.githubusercontent.com/luanti-org/luanti/master/luanti.conf.example
  echo -e "## Server settings generated by pelican\nname\nserver_name\nserver_description\nserver_address\nserver_url\nserver_announce\nserverlist_url\nmotd\nmax_users\nbind_address\ndefault_password\ndefault_game\n\n## Custom server settings\n" >> "$CONFIG_FILE"
fi

# Create games folder
GAMES_FOLDER=/mnt/server/.luanti/games
if [ -d "$GAMES_FOLDER" ]; then
  echo "GAMES folder already exists."
else 
  echo "GAMES folder does not exist. Making one..."
  mkdir -p "$GAMES_FOLDER"
fi

# Create mods folder
MOD_FOLDER=/mnt/server/.luanti/mods
if [ -d "$MOD_FOLDER" ]; then
  echo "Mods folder already exists."
else 
  echo "Mods folder does not exist. Making one..."
  mkdir -p "$MOD_FOLDER"
  curl -sSL -o "$MOD_FOLDER/mods_here.txt" https://raw.githubusercontent.com/luanti-org/luanti/master/mods/mods_here.txt
fi

# Install Gamemode
if [ "$LUANTI_SERVER_KIND" != "instance" ] && [ "$COMMUNITY_DOWNLOAD" = "1" ]; then
  if ! [ -z "$COMMUNITY_GAME_NAME" ]; then
    if ! [ -z "$COMMUNITY_GAME_AUTOR" ]; then
      echo "Download $COMMUNITY_GAME_NAME"
      D_URL=$(curl -s https://content.luanti.org/packages/$COMMUNITY_GAME_AUTOR/$COMMUNITY_GAME_NAME/ | grep -i download | grep packages | grep download | grep -o 'href=".*"' | cut -d "=" -f2- | egrep title= | awk -F' ' '{print $1}' | tr -d '"')
      curl -sSL -o /mnt/server/.luanti/games.zip https://content.luanti.org/$D_URL >/dev/null 2>&1
      unzip -o /mnt/server/.luanti/games.zip -d /mnt/server/.luanti/games >/dev/null 2>&1
      rm /mnt/server/.luanti/games.zip
    else
      echo "The COMMUNITY_GAME_AUTOR variable is required to download a community game"
    fi
  else
    echo "The COMMUNITY_GAME_NAME variable is required to download a community game"
  fi
fi

# Done!
echo "Installation was successfully completed!"

variables.name: Server kind
variables.description: Use standard for a normal server, or instance to clone a preset from a mounted template directory during installation.

variables.name: Instance template name
variables.description: Template subdirectory to copy from INSTANCE_TEMPLATE_MOUNT when LUANTI_SERVER_KIND is instance.

variables.name: Instance template mount
variables.description: Mount target inside the container that contains your preset Luanti server directories.

variables.name: Announce serverlist
variables.description: Announce to this serverlist.

variables.name: Community download
variables.description:
Download a community game from content.luanti.org.
Needs COMMUNITY_GAME_NAME and COMMUNITY_GAME_AUTOR

variables.name: Community game author
variables.description:
Case-sensitive!
Example: https://content.luanti.org/packages/Wuzzy/mineclone2/
then this should be Wuzzy

variables.name: Community game name
variables.description:
Case sensitive!
Example: https://content.luanti.org/packages/Wuzzy/mineclone2/
then this should be mineclone2

variables.name: Description of the server
variables.description: Description of server, to be displayed when players join and in the serverlist.

variables.name: Domain name of the server
variables.description: Domain name of server, to be displayed in the serverlist.

variables.name: Game name
variables.description: Default game when creating a new world. Only change if you have already uploaded the game!

variables.name: User data path
variables.description: Luanti user data directory. Keep this at /home/container/.luanti unless you are intentionally moving all server data.

variables.name: Game PATH
variables.description: Additional game search path. Defaults to the server-local .luanti/games directory.

variables.name: Max Players
variables.description: Maximum number of players that can be connected simultaneously.

variables.name: message of the day
variables.description: Message of the day displayed to players connecting.

variables.name: Name of the admin player.
variables.description: When running a server, clients connecting with this name are admins.

variables.name: Server name
variables.description: Name of the server, to be displayed when players join and in the serverlist.

variables.name: Server password
variables.description: New users need to input this password.

variables.name: Enable mod channels
variables.description: Enable Luanti modchannels so server-side bridge mods can talk to mt-multiserver-proxy.

variables.name: Server url
variables.description: Homepage of server, to be displayed in the serverlist.

variables.name: Show in server list
variables.description: Automatically report to the serverlist.

variables.name: World name
variables.description: The name of the world

variables.name: Shared mods path
variables.description: Additional mod search path inside the container (e.g. /home/mods). Mount a shared directory here via Pelican mounts so all servers share the same mods.

variables.name: Mod data path
variables.description: Directory used by container startup helpers for mod-owned persistent data, including classroom pending settings.
